paragraphs.body: My name is Daniel Thomas and I’m a student athlete at the University of Pittsburgh. I recently read and enjoyed, “Son of Mr. Green Jeans, an essay of Fatherhood,” which focused on the image of fatherhood not being as elevated as it should be.

I noticed that you effectively used great examples of contrasting ideas to support your idea of fatherhood not being as realistic as its portrayed in the public eye. For example, I was drawn to the paragraph where you mentioned the emperor penguins and how they became ‘ecstatic’ at the sight of their egg. Then the following paragraph mentioned how an average father had attempted suicide. This was very impactful as it shows the two sides to Fatherhood, the side that the world sees and the side that stays hidden.

annotations.text: This was very impactful as it shows the two sides to Fatherhood, the side that the world sees and the side that stays hidden.
annotations.comment: This is a great observation that I would love to see you explore more! For instance, you've done great work noticing the two sides of fatherhood in the essay, and I'd love to see you make the connections between that notion and your everyday life--do you also have these two sides? What about your family members? you teammates, coaches, peers, others who have influenced you in life?

paragraphs.body: Reading your essay, allowed me to reflect on my own past with fatherhood. It made me realise how fortunate I am to be around great fathers my whole life. Witnessing what my grandfather did for his 8 children and how it shaped my father into the man he is today. My grandfather made a huge effort to move his children out of the war torn country of Sudan. This had a huge impact on my father when he decided to move to the UK with my mother before they started our family. My father had always used tough love as a form of motivation to strive for more. I am grateful for everything he has done for me and my siblings but I never tell him that enough.

annotations.text: Witnessing what my grandfather did for his 8 children and how it shaped my father into the man he is today. My grandfather made a huge effort to move his children out of the war torn country of Sudan.
annotations.comment: Thank you for sharing this very personal detail from your own family's experiences. I am looking forward to seeing you explore how this generational event has shaped who you are!

paragraphs.body: This essay is pretty unconventional and I just want to know what led you to choose a newspaper style format. It is like nothing I have ever seen before and I am pretty curious about it.
Thank you for sharing this essay that made me realise how much my father and grandfather had done for. I will be sure to let them know how grateful I am for them Kind Regards, Daniel Thomas

annotations.comment: Great work here! Your making some really solid connections between this writing and your own observations/experiences. I'm really looking forward to seeing you explore these connections even more--I'd love to get to know you even better through your analysis of this writing!
